demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach has blown up so tight I cannot breathe when I lie down, and there is a constant, tearing pain deep in my pelvis.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last two days my belly has swelled up really fast and now I can't lie flat without gasping, and the deep pain low down is unbearable.

symptom_narrative.onset: a vague, dragging lower-tummy ache started about three months ago — I put it down to indigestion
symptom_narrative.duration: three months of the ache, but the swelling and breathlessness blew up over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: a constant, tearing, deep pelvic pain, with a bloated, stretched-tight feeling across the whole belly
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is the worst it has ever been, and I feel like I'm suffocating when I lie back
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: deep in the pelvis and across the whole lower belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the ache has been there for months and never really eases; the swelling and breathlessness have got dramatically worse in the last two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting bolt upright in my recliner is the only way I can breathe
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying flat makes the breathlessness far worse, and eating even a little makes the fullness and pain worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I fill up after just a few bites, I've lost about 5 kg without trying even though my belly is bigger, and I've been needing to pass urine much more often this past week
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before — I thought the ache was just my stomach playing up
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in the recliner, I can't lie in bed at all, and I'm frightened I'll fall because I feel so weak and off-balance

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no serious health problems that I know of — I always just put the tummy ache down to indigestion

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, no one else at home; worried about falling because of how weak and unsteady she feels and the change in her balance from the swollen belly

objective_data_offered.value: abdomen looks hugely swollen, tight and shiny, sticking right out
breathing looks fast and shallow, and she is sitting bolt upright leaning back in a recliner, unable to lie flat
both ankles look puffy and swollen
about 5 kg of weight lost over the last few months without trying, even though the belly keeps getting bigger
can only manage a few bites of food before feeling completely full
